zh.lifeSummary: 當Agnes Bishop於1502年2月出生在英國賀特福爾比曉普也斯 斯托特福德時，她的父親Thomas Bisshop22歲，母親Agnes De Samon24歲。她於1525年與Thomas Chandler在英國賀特福爾比曉普也斯 斯托特福德結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1582年6月7日在英國貝德福爾亨洛福享年80歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭賀特福爾比曉普也斯 斯托特福德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Agnes Bishop于1502年2月出生在英国賀特福爾比曉普也斯 斯托特福德时，她的父亲Thomas Bisshop22岁，母亲Agnes De Samon24岁。她于1525年与Thomas Chandler在英国賀特福爾比曉普也斯 斯托特福德结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1582年6月7日在英国貝德福爾亨洛福享年80岁，埋葬在英国英格兰賀特福爾比曉普也斯 斯托特福德。

nameMeanings.name: Bishop
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English bissop, biscop, Old English bisc(e)op ‘bishop’, which comes via Latin from Greek episkopos ‘overseer’. The Greek word was adopted early in the Christian era as a title for an overseer of a local community of Christians, and has yielded cognates in every European language: French évêque, Italian vescovo, Spanish obispo, Russian yepiskop, German Bischof, etc. The word came to be applied as a surname for a variety of reasons, among them a supposed resemblance in bearing or appearance to a bishop, and selection as the ‘boy bishop’ on Saint Nicholas's Feast Day. In some instances the surname is from the rare Middle English (Old English) personal name Biscop ‘bishop’. As an Irish surname it is adopted for Mac Giolla Easpaig, meaning ‘servant of the bishop’ (see Gillespie ). In North America, this surname has absorbed, by assimilation and translation, at least some of continental European cognates, e.g. German Bischoff , Polish, Rusyn, Czech, and Slovak Biskup , Slovenian Škof (see Skoff ).

variants.name: Bischoff
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Erwin, Bernhard, Hans, Klaus, Lorenz, Alois, Armin, Arno, Dieter, Fritz.

German: from Middle High German bischof, Middle Low German bischop ‘bishop’, probably applied as a nickname for someone with a pompous manner or as a metonymic occupational name for someone in the service of a bishop. See also Bishop .

variants.name: Bispo
meanings: Portuguese and Galician: nickname from bispo ‘bishop’ (see Bishop ).

variants.name: Gillespie
meanings: Scottish and Irish (Donegal): shortened Anglicized form of Scottish Gaelic Mac Gille Easbuig, borrowed into Irish as Mac Giolla Easpaig, a patronymic from a personal name meaning ‘servant of the bishop’.

variants.name: Episcopo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Orazio, Pasquale, Rocco.

Italian: from episcopo ‘bishop’ (from Latin episcopus (see Bishop ), probably a nickname for someone who had a pious manner or perhaps a metonymic occupational name for someone in the service of a bishop).

variants.name: Dean
meanings: English: topographic name from Middle English dene ‘valley’ (Old English denu), or a habitational name from any of several places in various parts of England named Dean or Dene from this word.

English: nickname or occupational name for the servant of a dean or nickname for someone thought to resemble a dean. A dean was an ecclesiastical official, the head of a chapter of canons or a church official with jurisdiction over a sub-division of an archdeaconry. Though no doubt some deans had illegitimate children, they were officially celibate, and in the main the surname is probably a nickname in origin, similar to Bishop , Prior , Priest , and Monk . The Middle English word deen, dien, dein, is a borrowing of Old French d(e)ien, doien from Latin decanus (originally a leader of ten men, from decem ‘ten’), and thus is a cognate of Deacon .
English: from the Middle English personal name Deyne (or Dene) a rhyming pet form of Reynald (see Reginald ).

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes Bishop was born in February 1502, in Bishop's Stortford, Hertfordshire, England, her father, Thomas Bisshop, was 22 and her mother, Agnes De Samon, was 24. She married Thomas Chandler in 1525, in Bishop's Stortford, Hertfordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 7 June 1582, in Henlow, Bedfordshire, England, at the age of 80, and was buried in Bishop's Stortford, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Agnes Bishop est née en février 1502, à Bishop's Stortford, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Bisshop, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Agnes De Samon, avait 24 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Chandler en 1525, à Bishop's Stortford, Hertfordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 7 juin 1582, à Henlow, Bedfordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 80 ans, et a été enterrée à Bishop's Stortford, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Juni 1582 im Alter von 80 Jahren in Henlow, Bedfordshire, England und wurde in Bishop's Stortford, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Agnes Bishopが1502年2月にイギリスハートフォードシュアイアビショプ'ス ストルトフォードで生まれた時，父Thomas Bisshopは22歳，母Agnes De Samonは24歳であった。1525年にイギリスハートフォードシュアイアビショプ'ス ストルトフォードでThomas Chandlerと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1582年6月7日にイギリスベドフォードシュアイアヘンロウで80歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスハートフォードシュアイアビショプ'ス ストルトフォードにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes Bishop(이)가 1502년 2월 영국 허트퍼드실르 비숍'스 스토르트퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Bisshop(은)는 22세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agnes De Samon(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 1525년 영국 허트퍼드실르 비숍'스 스토르트퍼드에서 Thomas Chandler(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1582년 6월 7일 영국 베드퍼드실르 헨로에서 80세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 허트퍼드실르 비숍'스 스토르트퍼드에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnes Bishop родилась февраль 1502 г. в местности бишоп'с стортфорд, хиртфордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Thomas Bisshop было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Agnes De Samon, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Chandler в 1525 г. в местности бишоп'с стортфорд, хиртфордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла 7 июня 1582 г. в местности хенлоу, бедфордшир, Англия в возрасте 80 лет и была похоронена в местности бишоп'с стортфорд, хиртфордшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Agnes Bishop nació en febrero de 1502, en Bishop's Stortford, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas Bisshop, tenía 22 años y su mamá, Agnes De Samon, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Thomas Chandler en 1525, en Bishop's Stortford, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 7 de junio de 1582, en Henlow, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 80 años, y fue sepultada en Bishop's Stortford, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
